# SuperPaste

**Press Option+V. The right text appears in whatever you're typing into — generated by an AI model running entirely on your Mac.**

SuperPaste is a small native macOS app that captures the frontmost window when you hit a hotkey, sends the screenshot to a local vision-language model, and pastes the response at your cursor. No cloud round-trip. Your screen never leaves the device.

It is fully open source under the MIT license. A one-time **lifetime license** buys you the signed/notarized DMG, future updates, and support — anyone who'd rather compile from source gets the same software, free. There is no subscription, no telemetry, and no server-side anything.

> **Status:** active rewrite. The cloud version on `main` ships today; the on-device version lives on the `oss-local-pivot` branch and is being built out phase-by-phase. See [`/Users/briansparker/.claude/plans/let-s-take-a-fresh-sleepy-coral.md`](https://github.com/brianjsparker/superpaste/issues) tracking issue for status.

---

## How it works

1. You place your cursor in any text field.
2. You press **Option+V**.
3. SuperPaste captures the frontmost window, downscales it to 1024px, and feeds it to a local vision model.
4. The model writes the appropriate response — a Slack reply, an email, the next line of code, an answer to a question.
5. SuperPaste writes the result to the clipboard and synthesizes a `⌘V` to paste it where your cursor is.

The model lives in `~/Library/Application Support/SuperPaste/Models/` and is downloaded the first time you launch the app. You pick which one — three tiers covering 8GB, 16GB, and 32GB Macs.

## Requirements

- **macOS 14** or later
- **Apple Silicon** (M1 or newer)
- **Full Xcode** for building from source — Command Line Tools alone can't compile MLX's Metal shaders, the binary will build but throw "metallib not found" at first inference
- **16GB+ RAM** recommended for the default model (a smaller option works on 8GB)

## Install

### From the signed DMG (paid)

Buy a lifetime license, download the signed/notarized DMG, drag SuperPaste to Applications. macOS will let you launch it without any "unidentified developer" friction. License unlocks updates forever — there is no recurring charge.

→ Coming soon.

### From source (free)

```bash
git clone https://github.com/brianjsparker/superpaste
cd superpaste

# One-time toolchain check
./bin/check-toolchain.sh

# Build the app
./build.sh
```

The result lands at `SuperPaste.app` in the repo root. Drag it to `/Applications` if you want it there.

The first time you launch SuperPaste it will:
1. Ask for Screen Recording and Accessibility permission
2. Walk you through picking a local model (Qwen 2.5 VL 3B is the default, ~2GB download)
3. Download the model from HuggingFace

After that, hit Option+V anywhere.

## Models

| Model | Disk | RAM | Best for |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| SmolVLM-Instruct-4bit | ~0.4 GB | ~0.7 GB | 8 GB Macs |
| Qwen 2.5 VL 3B Instruct 4-bit *(default)* | ~2.0 GB | ~2.2 GB | 16 GB Macs |
| Qwen 2.5 VL 7B Instruct 4-bit | ~4.5 GB | ~5.0 GB | 32 GB+ Macs |

All models are 4-bit MLX quantizations pulled from the [`mlx-community`](https://huggingface.co/mlx-community) HuggingFace org. You can switch models any time in Settings.

## Privacy

- The model runs in-process on your Mac. Inference does not touch the network.
- The screenshot is held in memory long enough to encode it for the model, then discarded.
- License validation against Polar.sh is the only outbound network call after the model is installed, and it happens at most once every 30 days.
- No analytics, no telemetry, no crash reporting. If something goes wrong, please file an issue.

## Architecture

- **Swift / SwiftUI** native macOS app, built with SwiftPM via `xcodebuild`
- **[MLX Swift](https://github.com/ml-explore/mlx-swift-lm)** for in-process VLM inference on Apple Silicon (Metal-backed)
- **HuggingFace** as the model source; downloads cached to `~/Library/Application Support/SuperPaste/Models/`
- **CGEvent** tap for the hotkey, **NSPasteboard** + synthesized `⌘V` for the paste

There is no backend server. There used to be a Cloudflare Worker that proxied requests to Anthropic; it is being deleted as part of the open-source pivot.

## Contributing

PRs welcome. A few ground rules:

- No telemetry, ever. "Nothing leaves your Mac" is the pitch.
- No new external network calls without an explicit user opt-in.
- Keep the dependency tree small. MLX, swift-transformers, and swift-huggingface are the only large ones.
- Tests where they actually catch things; not for coverage's sake.

For substantial changes, please open an issue first so we can sort the design out before you write the code.
